text.title: VUI Testing Layers, Metrics, and Two-Tier Suite Architecture
text.summary: Five testing layers, key metrics per layer, and the two-tier (deterministic + LLM-judge) suite structure.

section.title: Testing Layers
section.p: Five layers in order of granularity: unit (intent recognition accuracy), integration (dialog flow completion), user (real-world usability), stress (noise, interruptions, edge cases), accessibility (accent diversity, speech impairment).

rule.statement: Track three accuracy tiers separately: ASR word-error rate, NLU intent+slot F1, and dialog task completion. A regression in only one tier tells you exactly where to look.
rule.rationale: Aggregating across tiers hides the root cause. ASR regression means transcription changed; NLU regression means intent model changed; dialog regression means state machine changed. Each has a different fix.

list.item: Intent accuracy: % correct intent detection per utterance
Task completion: % of users completing their goal end-to-end
Error rate: dialog failures per conversation
Word error rate (WER): ASR transcription accuracy
User satisfaction: post-interaction survey (CSAT or 1-5 scale)

rule.statement: Run a two-tier test suite: tier 1 = deterministic assertions over scripted utterance fixtures (intent, slot, dialog-state outcomes); tier 2 = LLM-judge scoring for naturalness, error recovery, and goal completion against a rubric. Never mix the two tiers' outputs into a single pass/fail count.
rule.rationale: Deterministic assertions catch regressions objectively. LLM-judge scoring evaluates qualitative properties that assertions cannot reach. Mixing them produces an opaque aggregate that hides which type of regression occurred.

rule.statement: Pin ASR model versions in CI. Provider-side ASR upgrades silently shift WER and break tests overnight.
rule.rationale: Cloud ASR providers update models without notice. A passing suite built against model v1 can fail entirely against model v2 with no code changes. Version-pin prevents silent shifts.
